entry.title: Re: ALRTCATDEF - How to change the error message format f...
entry.content: Hi Eric, i have done that. Copied that alert with and configured. Now, as before alert inbox is generation fine. but now in sost mail, i dont see mail body text, its just "Alert Id: ##01864##". Also, email subject is coming in german and its not the one which is configured in ALRTCATDEF in Message title tab. The Mail subject is just my alert Category name.I think there is a language issue. Can you please guide on this.

entry.title: How to change message status from RETRY to FAILED when using Event Mesh via AMQP sender adpater in C
entry.content:
Hello,

I have developed an integration flow in CPI that consumes the message from a queue created in Event Mesh (in configure section). In my main queue, I have set parameters as below -

and also, created a DMQ with config as below -

Into my iflow, i have used sender channel as AMQP adapter, and set below retry parameters -

Here I was testing by sending a test message via postman for failed scenario. When I trigger the message, it is picked by AMQP adapter, executed 5 times as per above setting. But at the end of the processing the final message status in CPI is set as RETRY instead of FAILED.

Please guide if any specific configuration is required here.  My requirement is to set the end message if error then final status should be FAILED or REJECTED. And also set some delay in retry without using any other retry mechanism like JMS/Datastore, etc. 

Thanks & Regards,

Himani Patil

entry.title: Re: Best practice for complex Management Reporting in S/4...
entry.content:
Hi @andreasti12,

1. Splitting a single G/L account by Cost Center or Functional Area

Solution:

In Group Reporting, this can be achieved by using substitution rules to split a single G/L account into multiple FS items based on the cost center or functional area.

For your reference, please review the blog shared below.
How to Distribute One G/L Account Balance to Multi... - SAP Community

Hope this helps.

Regards,
Srivally

entry.title: How Ghana’s Local Content Enforcement Is Changing SAP S/4HANA Solution Design in Oil & Gas
entry.content:
The gap between what sellers think local content means and how it is implemented in Ghana’s oil and gas sector is widening.

In February 2026, the Petroleum Commission of Ghana reiterated its push toward deeper local-content enforcement.

Under Ghana’s local content framework (L.I. 2204 and subsequent amendments), critical operational scopes are now reserved for Indigenous Ghanaian Companies (IGCs), with stricter expectations around equity participation, partnerships, and reporting.

This is no longer a broad policy decision.
It is enforced through structure and proof.

Higher equity barriers: while 5% equity is the floor for petroleum licenses, providing goods and services via joint venture often requires at least 10% IGC participation.

Strategic alliances are being enforced: foreign firms are increasingly pushed into partnerships to demonstrate real technology transfer, not passive ownership.

The evidence trail matters: reporting expectations in 2026 increasingly require auditable proof of in-country spend and local participation.

For enterprise sellers working on S/4HANA programs in Ghana’s upstream sector, this is starting to show up as a design constraint across the system landscape, not only a compliance checkbox.
 
Across S/4HANA (Finance, Procurement, Projects) and the Oil & Gas industry solution, requirements are becoming more explicit:
• Vendor master data must classify and validate Indigenous Ghanaian Company (IGC) status, ownership, and eligibility for reserved scopes
• Joint venture and partner structures must reflect enforceable equity participation, not just commercial agreements
• Procurement and contract allocation must be traceable to demonstrate compliance with reserved operational scopes
• Project and operational spend must distinguish in-country vs external value with audit-level precision
• Data lineage across procurement, projects and finance must be provable, not reconstructed
 
This is starting to surface early in deal cycles during solution design, data model validation, and compliance workshops not just post-implementation.
 
If you’re running SAP S/4HANA deals in Ghana’s upstream sector, are you accounting for how local content requirements will be modelled, tracked, and defended across your solution design? Has local content started to affect your deal timelines or how your solution is being evaluated?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Pack: What You Need to Know About SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T
entry.content:
You received a notice about Compatibility Pack use rights, and you're wondering what this means for your SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T solution? This blog post walks you through what's happening, who's affected, and what you need to do in order extend your use right and move to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

 

What is a Compatibility Pack?

A Compatibility Pack is a limited use right that lets you run certain classic SAP ERP solutions on your SAP S/4HANA installation. It's essentially a bridge - a way to keep using older solutions while you transition to newer ones.

Your SAP ERP, add-on for Polish SAF-T regulatory requirements is part of this Compatibility Scope.

Who's Impacted?

You're affected if you're running SAP S/4HANA on-premise and using the classic SAP ERP add-on for Polish SAF-T. Here's the timeline you need to know: End of use right: May 31, 2026. SAP announced a final transition period, moving the expiration date from December 31, 2025, to May 31, 2026. That gives you breathing room, but not unlimited time.

Important note: This only applies to Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA systems. If you're running SAP ERP  in ECC  or SAP ERP in Private Cloud Edition, Compatibility Packs don't exist in those environments, this notice doesn't apply to you.

What Happens After May 31, 2026?

After May 31, 2026, you'll no longer be authorized to use the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T in your SAP S/4HANA on-premise system. Let me be clear about what this means:

This is a legal compliance matter. You might technically be able to keep using the software, but you won't have the legal right to do so. During audits, this becomes a problem. SAP also reserves the right to discontinue maintenance for customers without proper use rights.

You'll still be paying for it. Keeping an active license means ongoing charges for a solution you're not legally permitted to use. Reach out to your account owner to terminate your license as soon as possible.

No path forward without action. The Compatibility Pack was always meant as a temporary bridge. After May 31, 2026, that bridge closes.

How to Get an Extension

You have two main paths to extend your use rights beyond May 31, 2026. Let's look at both:

Path 1: RISE with SAP Contract

If you sign a RISE with SAP contract, your Compatibility Pack use rights extends. Extension starts from your RISE contract sign date. Your account owner will help you understand the criteria and move forward while keeping your use right for SAP ERP, add-on for Polish SAF-T regulatory requirements.

Path 2: Subscribe to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

This path extends your use rights - but only for the specific Compatibility Pack you're addressing. You must sufficiently subscribe to the respective corresponding SAP Cloud service of the Compatibility Pack functionality, which is one of the following SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solution version:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition tailored option (PTO)

Read more about supplemental terms and conditions for SAP Document and Reporting cloud services >>

One more critical date: The SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T reaches End of Life on December 31, 2027. After that date, SAP will deliver no legal changes. Plan your transition accordingly.

Why This Matters Now

You might be thinking: "Can I just wait, and see?" That's not a good strategy. The May 31, 2026 deadline is firm. After that, you're operating without proper use rights. That's a compliance risk you don't want. The transition takes time. Moving to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance isn't something you do overnight. You need time for planning, implementation, and testing.

You have options, but they require action. Both exemption paths need contracts signed and requirements met. The sooner you start, the smoother your transition.

Next Steps

Here's what I recommend you do right now:

  1. Review your current setup

    • Confirm you're using SAP S/4HANA on-premise with the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T
    • Check your contract end dates
    • Understand how many documents/entities you're processing

  2. Contact your SAP account team

    • Discuss which exemption path makes sense for your business
    • Start planning your transition timeline to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  3. Explore SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

    • Learn how it works with your current setup
    • Understand the implementation effort and the functionalities the solution offers (you might you is already for other countries and regions)
  1. Plan your migration
    • Set realistic timelines
    • Capacity and investments for the transition
    • Involve your IT team early

Resources to Help You

SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Note 3096074 - Details for SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T customers
  • SAP Note 3593194 - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Solution Strategy for SAF-T reporting (JPK) in Poland (SAP S/4HANA)

Additional Information:

  • SAP Community Blog - Compatibility Scope: What happens after expiry of use rights?
  • SAP News - Official announcement about the transition period
  • SAP Help Portal - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - Learn about the go-forward solution
  • SAP Help Portal - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - SAF-T Poland

The Bottom Line

The Compatibility Pack was always a temporary bridge, not a permanent solution. You've got until May 31, 2026, before use rights expire. You have clear paths forward - through RISE with SAP or by subscribing to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Both options give you extended use rights, but both require action on your part.

Your SAP account team is ready to help you navigate this transition. Reach out to them, review your options, and make a plan. You've kept your business compliant with Polish SAF-T requirements this long. Let's make sure you stay compliant through this transition and beyond.

 

Questions about your specific situation? Contact your SAP account team - they can provide personalized guidance based on your contracts and business needs.

entry.title: Strange behaviour in SQL Remote
entry.content:
I have two databases, one publicating data to the other database (using dbremote).

For some reason, the publisher is sending messages, the remote database reads the messages (and also purge them) but doesnt apply them (I see in the log "Received message from...." but I am missing the line with "Applying message from....").

 

What could be the reason for such a behaviour?

entry.title: Re: Cannot create variant with UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service and Fiori Elements
entry.content: Hi, have you found the solution? I have now the same problem in CF.

entry.title: Alternative cost center for a wage type generated during a payroll run
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We have received a requirement in which a wage type that is generated directly during a payroll run needs to be assigned to a cost center different from the one specified in Infotype 0001.

We are aware that an alternative cost center can be maintained via Infotype 15. However, since the wage type in question is only created during payroll processing, this option is unfortunately not suitable for our use case.

Are there any other options or places in the system where this assignment can be made?

We have considered adjusting this in the coding, but so far have not been able to identify in which report or at which point the assignment to the cost center takes place.

Does anyone have any ideas or possible solutions?

Thank you very much in advance!

Kind regards,

Olga

entry.title: Creation of Alternative GL Accounts in YACN chart of accounts
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have recently created additional G/L accounts for our client within the standard chart of accounts YCOA, which is currently in use.

We observed that each of these G/L accounts is associated with an alternative G/L account from the local chart of accounts YACN. In this context, we intended to create new alternative G/L accounts within YACN and link them to the newly created G/L accounts in YCOA.

However, it appears that we are unable to create new G/L accounts in YACN, as the save option is inactive. Could you please confirm whether it is permitted in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to create new G/L accounts in the local chart of accounts YACN? Alternatively, should we select and assign from the standard alternative G/L accounts already available in YACN?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Claudia

entry.title: Auto Fill Serial Numbers in Outbound Delivery
entry.content: Business Object which can be used to auto fill serial numbers in Outbound Delivery through a custom program in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud. Facing issue with not able to fill the serial number.

entry.title: How to: Create Job Information Records Based on a Custom Date Field
entry.content:
During one of my projects, the following business requirement was identified:

Automatically create a Job Information record with an effective date driven by a custom date field.

Solution Approach

To address this requirement, a scheduled integration was configured using SuccessFactors Integration Center. The implementation details are described below.

1. Job Information Configuration

  • A custom date field named Next Change Date was added to the Job Information entity.
  • This field represents the date on which a new Job Information record should be created by the integration.

2. Integration Center Setup

A new integration was created in Integration Center with the following configuration:

  • Trigger Type: Scheduled
  • Source Type: SuccessFactors
  • Destination Type: SuccessFactors
  • Format: OData v2

2.1 Entity Selection

  • Selected Job Information (EmpJob) as the integration entity.

2.2 Field Mapping

  • In the Configure Fields step, the custom field Next Change Date was mapped to the Event Date field, ensuring the new record is created with the correct effective date.

2.3 Detail View Configuration

  • Relevant settings were adjusted in the Detail View to enable record creation based on the effective date logic.

 

 

2.4 Filtering Criteria

  • A filter was configured to ensure that only Job Information records where Next Change Date equals the current date are selected for processing.

 

2.5 Scheduling and Execution

  • The integration was scheduled and executed according to the defined frequency.

Validation and Results

  • Upon execution, Job Information records were reviewed.
  • Navigate to Job Information for the affected employee(s).
  • Confirm that:
    • A new Job Information record has been created
    • The effective date corresponds to the Next Change Date
    • In this example, a new Job Information record was successfully created with an effective date of 13 April 2026, confirming the expected behavior
  •  

The integration successfully automates the creation of effective‑dated Job Information records based on a custom date field, ensuring timely and consistent data updates without manual intervention.

entry.title: Re: ALRTCATDEF - How to change the error message format f...
entry.content: Hey Eric, I created the same SO10 texts into DE language. And now it is working as expected. So now my ALRTCATDEF is in EN language and email triggering from AIF action FM is on DE language i guess. My one concern is, for now I created SO10 texts in DE language. But suppose, user might be from other language other than DE or EN right? In this case how can I make my alert work or basically make it dynamic?

entry.title: ABAP Developer 752: DB NPL error 'default' segment full although enough freespace available
entry.content:
Good morning,

I have a mysterious issue:

Just installed the ABAP developer trial 752 on VBox and openSuse. Installation finished, everything seems to be fine.

But I get error messages in ASE log immediately after starting SAP instance, indicating that there isn't enough space in data device. E.g. "Can't allocate space for object 'DDLOG' in database 'NPL' because 'default' segment is full/has no free extents".

I added space to the device (disk resize ...) and assigned it to the database (alter database ...). The sp_helpdb command shows plenty of free space, but still get the above error???

Can anybody help?

Regards, Manfred

'Something went wrong. The system couldnt load the custom widget for this reason: Integrity hash must not be empty and must start with one of 'sha256', 'sha384', or 'sha512', followed by a '-' character and the actual hash value.'.

Hopefully someone can confirm that he or she can still reproduce the steps described in the blog and if some adjustments need to be made.

Best regards,
Josh

entry.title: Re: Event-driven architecture
entry.content:
Thanks, @VijayKonam  — completely agree that AEM and Integration Suite are integration layers and shouldn’t carry business logic. Let me clarify what I meant by “multi‑consumer” because the issue I’m dealing with is a bit complex than simple transaction posting. since I am from Utilities industry, means residential/ industrial / street light/small shops customer. 

My scenario is: one business event → multiple downstream behaviors
Think of something like a PizzaHut order event, but with different consumers needing different slices of the same event:

  • Customer A: updates order status in S/4.
  • Customer B: triggers a delivery workflow.
  • Customer C: enriches the event with customer preferences and pushes it to a loyalty engine
  • Customer sends a real‑time notification to the mobile app
  • Customer E: stores the raw event for analytics / replay

Every customer has different latency expectations, different retry semantics, and different enrichment needs. Where the boundary question comes in

AEM gives me:

  • topic hierarchy
  • fan‑out
  • filtering
  • replay
  • durable queues
  • hybrid routing

But AEM won't  give me:

  • domain validation
  • enrichment (e.g., fetch customer tier, fetch delivery SLA)
  • idempotency rules
  • compensating logic
  • orchestration across multiple systems
  • API exposure for downstream apps
  • business‑specific error handling

That’s where I’m trying to draw the line between, since this is my problem statement:

  1. AEM = event backbone
  2. CAP/iFlow = domain consumer boundary

The Major architectural question I’m exploring for multi‑consumer patterns at scale, please suggest if you have insight ,do you:

  • Let AEM do all routing/fan‑out, and keep each consumer (CAP/iFlow) extremely thin?
  • Introduce a domain‑specific consumer service (e.g., CAP) that performs enrichment + validation before fanning out to other systems?
  • Use AEM queues directly per consumer, and let each consumer handle its own logic independently?
  • Use Integration Suite as the “first consumer” and then orchestrate downstream flows?

This is important for me since I’m trying to avoid and client do not want:

  • duplicated logic across consumers
  • tight coupling
  • consumers re‑implementing the same enrichment
  • brittle retry chains

So I’m willing to understand this is little weird requirement and if anyone approached this boundary in real projects — especially when the same event must drive multiple, heterogeneous downstream actions. 

Appreciate suggestion.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub

entry.title: Manage Project Billing Requests - Approver does not get exported to Excel
entry.content: When using Manage Project Billing Requests, when Approver is added to the results grid, it is not exported to excel when the export button  is pressed.

entry.title: How to make ERT serial number to retain the manufacturer’s leading zero in ECC and SSAM
entry.content:
The business requirement is to retain the manufacturer’s leading zero in the ERT serial number, so that the value stored in SAP matches the physical device and supports accurate processing in both ECC and SSAM.

  • Whether retaining leading zeros in this scenario is possible within SAP ECC standard/supportable configuration
  • If not, whether the recommended solution should instead be handled in the SAP SSAM/UI layer
  • Which approach is considered SAP-supported and upgrade-safe, with minimal impact to future SSAM updates and upgrades

Appreciate you confirming the SAP-supported, upgrade-safe approach that minimizes impact to future SSAM updates and upgrades.

Steps to reproduce: 

  1. Login to SSAM app.
  2. Choose the work order, Start the work order
  3. Scroll down to Parts
  4. Choose Part -> Select + icon to 'Issue Part'
  5. Scan a barcode of the ERT material.
  6. Observe the material number populated is with a leading zero.
  7. Search result is empty because the materials in ECC does not display the leading zero in the display format. So, when leading zero is manually removed in after the barcode scan, the search result displays  the materials serial numbers for selection.

entry.title: Re: SPROXY shows “Service outdated” only for newly created Integration Suite Service Interfaces
entry.content: Hello @radhika_javvaji, the version is "Cloud Integration 6.77.32" "Cloud Integration Runtime 8.41.14" KR, Stefan

entry.title: Re: Recurrency execution order
entry.content: by defining 2 different parameters in the rate plan (set it as optional and set a value with the expected second ). Then the parameter will be available in the list like SAP_BILL_CYCLE_DATE

entry.title: Re: Budget Availability Control Including Tax in PO – SAP...
entry.content:
To my knowledge, in Public Cloud, AVC is designed to work with the committed values from POs, which by default include tax. Unlike on-premise, there is no SSCUI or customizing option to exclude tax from budget consumption. The flexibility is intentionally limited in Public Cloud to keep processes standardized.
The recommended practice is to plan budgets including expected tax amounts.

Could someone please guide me on how to register Bank Groups using this function?

Specifics:

  • Format: We are using the Japanese format "JP1_JBA (Zenginkyo)".

  • Reference Community No: 13998932

Any advice, step-by-step instructions, or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Non‑Cash Customer Benefits Using SAP Settlement Management
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement from the client to provide "no cash" benefits to customer using settlement management. Such benefits are usually externally procured services. For example end customer receives Amazon gift card or loyality trip at the end of the settlement. Accruals should be still collected from sales orders and "consumed" against vendor invoices.

What is the best practice for such processes in SAP?

entry.title: Re: Event-driven architecture
entry.content:
Thanks for the details Atul.

AEM should be your brokering layer. Topics, Queues, Subscriptions and Fanouts. If needed replays as well but I really doubt that is needed for transaction and becomes complex if you need to replay only for few consumers.

SAP-IS could be your consumer for orchestration, enrichment, workflow triggering agent etc. Unless you have a user interaction at UI level (not workflow) I do not see a need for CAP application. These are mainly for UI applications in my mind. You need a strong service layer probably using APIM as the single point of exposure with strong security and other controls implemented. These services should/could be  idempotent and take care of that one individual process, operation or transaction etc. Everything should come together. 

If you see you have two consumers with same enrichment, the logic should never be duplicated. Instead this one consumer (IS or Service) should do that and then post another message to a topic that broadcasts it to the end consumers.

For data and analytics related consumers where you need to have replay possibility probably they all should work out of a single topic that broadcasts the event or message. This way you do not have to worry about replays. Segregating analytical and transactional consumers is a must in my mind.

This is how I would design my layers and segregate them with specific job duties.

Hope to discuss more if you have any specific use case to go end to end!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Configuration in S4HANA system (on-premise)

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP Platform Automation #3 (April Edition)
entry.content:
📣The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for btp CLI or Terraform on SAP BTP send me an email and we will get back to you.

Introduction

In this monthly series we show and discuss all things around automating SAP BTP with the btp CLI or the Terraform providers from SAP.

📼You find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this on this GitHub page

Meeting Information

When?

  • May 12th, 11:00 – 12:00 CET – Teams Webinar Registration (please register in advance)

Who?

  • All interested customer, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • btp CLI and Terraform Team

 

What’s up (aka preliminary agenda)?

  • What’s new in April
  • Demos
    • Terraform provider authentication via btp CLI
    • Terraform provider for Cloud Connector
  • Q + A

📋  Register here 

✨Looking forward to meeting you!

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA 2020 Upgrade On Premise - S4 HANA 2023 or S4...
entry.content:
Hello shareeq,

the update procedure is the same and also the effort will be the identical for both updates.

so i would suggest to go on s4 2025

best regards

qwertie

entry.title: Re: How to register Bank Groups via Bank Master Data Cock...
entry.content:
Hello @RYOGA_HOSHI 

To register Bank Groups in SAP’s Bank Master Data Cockpit using the Japanese JP1_JBA (Zenginkyo) format, you need to configure the country-specific settings, upload the JBA file, and process it through the cockpit’s import functions. The cockpit centralizes all bank master tasks, including group registration, so the key is ensuring the JP1_JBA format is correctly mapped in customizing before upload.

Thanks!!!

BR

Manas

entry.title: Setting Your SAP TM and SAP EWM Project Up for Success – Join Our Continuous Quality Check Pilots!
entry.content:
Are you looking to ensure your SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) and SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM) implementation projects are on the right path? Would you like to gain clarity on critical topics, best practices, and potential challenges before they impact your project timeline?

If so, we have excellent news for you!

SAP Enterprise Support, via the Enterprise Support Advisory Council, is excited to announce two new pilot offerings designed to help your organization achieve implementation excellence through our Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for transformation assessment for SAP TM and SAP EWM.

About the Pilots:

The CQC for transformation assessment is conducted during the SAP Activate "Prepare" phase. This service delivers:

  • Evaluation of your project team's readiness by assessing awareness and knowledge of critical SAP TM and SAP EWM topics
  • Gap analysis SAP-recommended approaches
  • Individualized recommendations, including best practices, enablement resources, and actionable guidance
  • Strategic clarity on key implementation topics, common project challenges, integration requirements, and system considerations

Whether you're implementing SAP TM, SAP EWM, or both solutions together, this assessment will provide valuable insights to strengthen your project foundation, covering essential content areas such as:

  • System availability and performance
  • Project preparation
  • Solution-specific best practices

Who Should Participate?

This pilot is ideal for organizations that:

  • Are in the SAP Activate "Prepare" phase for SAP TM and/or SAP EWM
  • Have assembled a project team ready to begin or currently planning their implementation
  • Want to validate their implementation approach against SAP best practices

Ready to Transform Your Implementation?

If your organization meets the criteria above, don't miss this opportunity to join a new pilot service designed to maximize your SAP investment and ensure project success.

Reach out today to the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council for detailed information and take the first step toward a successful, efficient implementation.

We look forward to partnering with you on this exciting journey to operational excellence.

More information:

  • SAP TM - short video here.
  • SAP EWM - short video here.

Get involved? Be part of the innovation!

  • Visit ESAC on SAP Support Portal
  • contact us:  esac@sap.com

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. Value maps on SAP Community bring together guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next - all included in your SAP cloud subscription.
Watch our short demo video, then head over to our page to explore our range of topics and request a call with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.
Have questions or feedback on this content? Feel free to leave a comment below.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Key Onboarding Release
entry.content: Hi - regarding OBX-39493 "Form I-9 Guardrail Message for Document Receipt Requirements for E-Verify" - I am wondering, how are companies supposed to document that they have reviewed a receipt within the 3-day timeline? I understand that E-Verify doesn't accept receipts, so it makes sense that we can't do an E-Verify until the original document is received, but we still need to have documentation that we reviewed the receipt within the 3 day timeline, don't we? If we can't even select a receipt and progress to sign section 2, it will look like we did not meet the deadline of reviewing the receipt within 3 days.

entry.title: Configuration Define instruction key is not found
entry.content: I need to create a new instruction key for payment files; however, I am unable to locate the configuration “Define Instruction Key” in Central Business Configuration.

entry.title: app " Schedule Tax Jobs" public cloud
entry.content:
We're writing regarding the following issue encountered on the app "Schedule Tax Jobs".

Our objective is using the Job Template "Advance Return for Tax" to make monthly prints of VAT Register automatic, and in particular we need to launch the job with a template where it is possible to exclude some "Document Types" with the Dynamic Selections.

The problem is that, while the Dynamic Selections are available in the app "Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchases", they seem not available in the Job Template.

In addition, we tried with the Job Template "Additional List for Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchases" but here there is no Document Numbering section, so it is not possible to update registers numbers accordingly.

Could you please advise on the topic?

entry.title: Searching for Best Practise of Creating a material masta data
entry.content:
I'm looking for a best practice approach in SAP Signavio or on SAP for Me for creating a material master data.
Can anyone tell me where I can find such an approach?

entry.title: Strange behavior with EML for BO i_prodallocationobjecttp_2
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am using business object i_prodallocationobjecttp_2 to create or update time series with below EML statements and it is working perfect as I expected in SE38 report.

The strange thing is not working when copy same logic to class method which is going to call under Batch job in S4 scheduled from build workzone catalog. Logic written in IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT~EXECUTE.

For Time series , I am trying with below logic in both places SE38 and SE24

Update timeseries:

APPEND VALUE #(
%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off
prodallocationtimeseriesuuid = <fs_pal_update>-prodallocationtimeseriesuuid
%control-productallocationquantity = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
productallocationquantity = <fs_pal_update>-productallocationquantity
) TO lt_ts_upd.

"Execute action
MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_prodallocationobjecttp_2 "IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY prodallocobjtimeseries
UPDATE FROM lt_ts_upd
FAILED ls_eml_failed
REPORTED ls_eml_reported.

Create timeseries:

APPEND VALUE #(
%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off
%cid_ref = ' ' "sy-index "always empty because characteristic value combination was created earlier
charcvaluecombinationuuid = <fs_pal_create>-charcvaluecombinationuuid
%target = VALUE #(
(
%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off
%cid = sy-index "'311'
%control-prodallocperdstartutcdatetime = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
prodallocperdstartutcdatetime = <fs_pal_create>-prodallocationperiodstartdate "'20230101000000'
%control-prodallocperiodendutcdatetime = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
prodallocperiodendutcdatetime = <fs_pal_create>-prodallocperdstartutcdatetime "'20230201000000'
%control-productallocationquantity = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
productallocationquantity = <fs_pal_create>-productallocationquantity
)
)
) TO lt_ts_cba.

*-- Execute creation
MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_prodallocationobjecttp_2
ENTITY prodalloccharcvalcombn CREATE BY \_prodallocobjtimeseriestp
FROM lt_ts_cba
FAILED ls_eml_failed
REPORTED ls_eml_reported.

Commit after each modify

COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSE OF i_prodallocationobjecttp_2
FAILED ls_eml_failed_late
REPORTED ls_eml_reported_late.

After Modify EML also after commit sy-subrc is zero and there are no errors in EML_FAILED /  EML_REPORTED/  EML_FAILED_LATE/  EML_FAILED_REPORTED but my time series are not loading with SE24 and loading as expected in SE38.

Note: When I run SE38 program in batch job it is working as expected .

View I am checking: I_PRODALLOCOBJTIMESERIESTP_2 to validate my timeseries loaded or not.

Please help me, I am really clue less now after all tries and couldn't understand difference as the logic is same in both places (Copy pasted logic).

Thanks,

Sandeep

entry.title: TPM to send 997 converts to status idoc and back to SAP
entry.content: From B2B partner we are receiving 997 functional acknowledgements to CPI , but we don't find any option to route this through mapping to the ECC system. Any inputs will be highly appreciated.Thanks

entry.title: Story 2.0 -SAC Story running for Saved date
entry.content:
Story 2.0 -SAC Story running for Saved date

 

When we used the URL with no filter value applied, the HANA variable would work successfully

When we used the URL with a filter value applied, the HANA variable would not run.

entry.title: Recurring supplier invoice: Fill in mandatory field INVOICE_DOC_ITEM (table parameter GLACCOUNTDATA,
entry.content:
Hello,

when trying to execute a recurring supplier invoice in SAP Public Cloud the following error message occurrs.

Fill in mandatory field INVOICE_DOC_ITEM (table parameter GLACCOUNTDATA, row 3)

The error message occurrs for 3 rows, although all 31 rows are exactly the same.

Can anybody help?

Kind regards

Fabian

entry.title: Re: Down Payment adjustment for Partial delivery/Partial...
entry.content:
Hi all,

Further to my question for user of down payment for partially delivered quantities, I found an old consulting note- 1788841 (last updated in 08.2024) which informs about condition based down payment processing in EhP 6.0 using condition AZWA and AZWB in the pricing procedures.

Has anyone made use of this functionality in S/4 HANA on Premise? what are the pitfalls that need to be taken into consideration apart from the ones highlighted by SAP in the note?

1788841 - Consulting note for condition-based down payments - SAP for Me

Any suggestions are welcome.

Thanks,

Kartik Shah

entry.title: Re: SSO Migration from ADFS to Entra ID. IAS – Guidance R...

The approach of introducing Microsoft Entra ID as a secondary Corporate IdP and validating it at the application level (LMS Preview) before any broader change makes a lot of sense and aligns well with our planned migration approach. We’ll proceed with reviewing this setup internally and begin preparing the LMS Preview configuration and testing. Appreciate the clear steps and best practice recommendations. Thanks again, Serguei

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Configuration in S4HANA system (on-premise)
entry.content:
@Hi @krishnachaitanyavukkala 

The issue was sorted out with the help of Firewall team for the on-prem systems. they whitelisted the Cloud ALM URL mentioned in the error and/or the corresponding port number.

Thanks for your response.

entry.title: Re: Oauth2 grant_type ''password" is not working after JD...
entry.content: I've read all the stuff and having same error too. The grant type is a custom one created and set into the public oauth client, also added authorizationserver.supported.grant.types=authorization_code,refresh_token,client_credentials,urn:dev:sso:ecom:fujitsu:com and <bean depends-on="customAuthorizationGrantTypes" parent="listMergeDirective"> <property name="add" value="urn:dev:sso:ecom:fujitsu:com" /> </bean>

entry.content: It's possible change BP address in moment to change Sales Order? Is necessary any more configuration? Because here the address not change in moment realize VA02. Can anybody help me?

If we consider that the automation is mainly gained by background jobs in SAP, then according to my investigation only about 18 % of tasks can possibly be automated. Is this also a market standard? Will all tracking of all other tasks remain manual?

entry.title: Modules << Business Partners << Batch Print OR Mail Customer Aging Letters(BUP20001) does not print
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

We are currently facing a system-wide issue in SAP Business One where Coresuite is not generating any outputs (Email, PDF, or Excel), for Modules << Business Partners << Batch Print OR Mail Customer Aging Letters(BUP20001). This is impacting multiple users and business operations.

(It shows Coresuite Designer: Email for few seconds, disappeared and nothing happens.)

Coresuite Designer is running in SAP, but no corresponding Windows service is present on the server. This indicates the backend runtime/engine is missing, which is causing all output functions (PDF/Excel/Email) to fail.

How could we solve this. Thanks you!

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Updated Home Page 2H 2025
entry.content: Greetings -- we are testing the new home page experience with the 1H 2026 upgrade and so far, it's not looking good.  There is no branding allowed and most of the legacy quick actions are missing with no way to add them back.  Something as simple as View My Profile.  This new home page isn't going to work if employees can't even view their profile.  SAP has gone from 10 quick actions on our legacy home page to allowing only 3 on the new.  This doesn't seem like an improved experience.

entry.title: Add custom field to engineering record view table
entry.content:
Hello,

i am currently facing a requirement to add the creation date of specifications to the view table as an additional field/column in engineering records. I extended the structure for object specific attributes with the creation date field for specifications and during the dynamic creation of the view table, i can see that the data for the new field was retrieved successfully by the feeder class. 

 

Unfortunately, this does not seem to be sufficient information for the dynamic creation of the table. I dont know where i have to tell the system to include my new field during the creation process. Can someone help me find the solution?

Here is a screenshot of the table (lower part of the screenshot), which should have my additional "creation date" field

Thank you very much in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Cloud Upgrade (Version 2026.2.6 – QRC 1/2026)
entry.content:
Dear Saikumar33

Merges are triggered either by another process implicitly (log replay, recovery) or through a merge decision function at regular intervals every 60s and might trigger a merge activity if certain thresholds are exceeded, or not. Shorter merge intervals would normally mean there is increased DML activity happening.

With QRC2 2025, the merge thread parallelism (how many table columns are merged at the same time) is 0, and therefore directly controlled by the instance size. 
If you experience long running merges as well, please consider upsizing. 

Neither the interval nor the decision function can be changed by customers, as these are optimized and maintained by SAP.

If you are interested in delta merges, to an extent you may reference the KBA 2057046 - FAQ: SAP HANA Delta Merges 
Be aware that most of the information there is for HANA Platform Edition, with some references to QRC changes in SAP HANA Cloud. The principle of the mechanism is almost the same.

If you are experiencing unexplainably short merge intervals, please reach out to SAP Support directly through a support case to troubleshoot your specific issue.

Please find specific (patch-) release information through the following resources: 

Patches and fixes (Patch releases): 
3716297 - SAP HANA Cloud 2026 QRC1 Patch Information

New features in SAP HANA Cloud (QRC release): 
https://help.sap.com/whats-new/2495b34492334456a49084831c2bea4e?Component=SAP+HANA+Cloud&Version=2026.2+(QRC+1/2026)

Kind regards, 
Oliver

entry.title: Valuation Class Change – Posting Period Restriction and Financial Impact Clarification
entry.content:
Description:

We are performing a mass valuation class change for materials with existing stock.

Current scenario:

- Stock is written off at the end of the period (e.g., April 30)
- Valuation class is updated
- System does not allow posting stock back in the same period after valuation class change
- Error received: "Back-posting only possible with same valuation class"

As a result:

- We are required to post stock back in the next period (e.g., May 1)
- This creates a timing difference where:
 - Inventory is reduced in April
 - Inventory is restored in May
- Finance reports show consumption in April and discrepancies in month-end closing

Questions:
1. Is there any supported approach to complete write-off and write-on within the same posting period when valuation class changes?
2. Are there SAP-recommended best practices to minimize financial reporting impact in this scenario?

I would appreciate confirmation and any recommended approach for handling this process in a way that aligns with financial reporting requirements.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 3694507 – Package Information Missing for Ma...
entry.content:
3694507 - [TDF SP20] Note 018: EFD-Reinf - Improvements for error handling of certificate errors - SAP for Me

The manual instruction says:

1. CREATE THE NEW ERROR MESSAGE
1.1 Go to t-code SE91.
1.2 Enter message classe: “/TMF/MF” and click on Change button.

So, the package is automatically the one of the existing message class (you must not enter a Z package).

If anything else happens, it means that there's something wrong in your installation, or the way you have installed the SAP notes or in this SAP note itself, so either diagnose by yourself or contact SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Recurrency execution order
entry.content: So, I'll have to create one parameter for each specific time I need, right?

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding enhancement to the Internal Hire process.
entry.content:
Hi,

I understood during the relase Q&A yesterday that the "enable internalhire process" will be turned on automatically in H2 2026. Will it be universal?

Can you confirm that when we go from recruiting to onboarding (crossboarding for internal hire) we are able to trigger a WF before the record is created in EC?

thank you!

AnnCh

entry.title: Re: mass adding storage loc to material master.
entry.content:
Hi,

MMSC_MASS should work, and you can even try with MM17 by choosing MARD table (new creation). If you are on SAP S4HANA you can also use mgiration cockpit object/template : Product - extend existing record by new org levels.

Regard.

entry.title: The cost center cannot be changed in the WBS element after financial plan data has been uploaded
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

It is not possible to change the cost center in a WBS element after financial plan data has been uploaded via the Import Financial Plan Data app. Example: WBS element xxxxx. However, it is also not possible to delete the financial plan data in the “Delete Financial Plan Data” app.

Is this behavior intended by SAP by design, or is there a bug in the application? How is it supposed to be possible to adjust the cost center despite the financial plan data?I look forward to your response.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The cost center cannot be changed in the WBS element...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Once a project is created it is no longer possible to edit the Company Code. Then once there are postings on a WBS/Project it is no longer possible to edit the Cost Center. As this is the current standard behavior please consider voting on Improvement Request https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/329790 if you would like to see this behavior changed in future releases.

Best regards, 

Tiago 
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Erro na nota fiscal de saída para serviço de obra
entry.content:
Estou com problema para enviar uma nota fiscal de saída para serviço de obras, a mensagem de erro que aparece é:

Erro: Rejeitado pela regras municipais: Para este tipo de prestação de serviço é obrigatório o preenchimento das Informações da Obra.#13; O Valor Líquido da Nota Fiscal não confere com o calculado. Valor incorreto: 128944,2. Valor esperado: 145829,75&|#

Obs: Utilizamos o SAP B1 e os Add-ons TipleOne LW do grupo Skill / TaxPlus MDFe da Invent

entry.title: Re: How to check the memory usage of a specific table in...
entry.content:
Hi, 

You can see the memory consumption from different areas of the table via the M_CS_TABLES view also.

Example :

SELECT  TABLE_NAME, PART_ID, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA, RECORD_COUNT, RAW_RECORD_COUNT_IN_MAIN, RAW_RECORD_COUNT_IN_DELTA,
FROM M_CS_TABLES WHERE TABLE_NAME = '<table name>'

Kind Regards
Simon

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Please find the Week 2 screenshots -

User:  AC300864U275
Dev Space: AC300864U275_GenAI_Challenge

entry.title: Avoid create sales order from certain sales office and invoice from certain business area
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community

Sales Order creation in VA01

I have Sales office1, Sales office2 and Sales office3 in the same sales organization.

Sales office1 and Sales office2 could create also sales orders in Sales office3. However, Sales office3 shouldn't create the sales order in the rest of the Sales offices.

We have a role that has V_VBAK_VKO with the sales organization and channel distribution.

We have try to add the sales office to block in VA01, but it hasn´t worked. It seems that it isn´t work in sales office level. Is it this correct or is there a way it can work?

I've seen the OSS note 3046057 - Authorization check regarding sales office and sales group in sales document. This note have an example to create a z authorization object. Can´t we manage it by authorizations in roles, without ABAP? It's just I wanted to know if there weren't any other way apart from using user-exit.

Invoice creation in VF01 and VF04

We have the same problem at invoice creation VF01 and VF04 transaction, how to manage the authorizations in roles to filter by business area? I have business area1 and 2 that can see BA3, but BA3 shouldn´t see BA1 and BA2. We are using V_VBRK_VKO. Is there a way to manage by standard authorization objects?

 

Kindly ask for your advice and thank you in advance.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: TPM to send 997 converts to status idoc and back to SAP
entry.content: I don't believe the solution supports that function. One of the handful of reasons we are using something different that I created.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Updated Home Page 2H 2025
entry.content:
Honestly, SAP should have informed us about the new RBPs and its functionality.

 

We literally had to reverse engineer a lot of things to know that new RBPs been introduced without knowing them and were preventing many areas from functioning properly.

 

i hope they learn from such mistakes as it is crucial and directly impact end users.

entry.title: Re: RFC_LOGON_FAILURE: SNC required for this connection f...
entry.content: This is totally working but in SAP Note 3338929 there is a missing information, that from default SAPup_add.par will be not included. To apply this you have to activate this in SAPup.par by adding this line "include_opt = SAPup_add.par"

entry.content:
Hi @AnnCh ,

Yes, you will get workflow approval to-do before the record is created in EC. 

Thanks,

Smita

entry.title: Re: What is the recommended approach for vendor-integrati...
entry.content: Yes, our instance was provisioned after March 3. We are looking to create an integration with the Background Check provider Mintz

entry.title: Request for Bulk Download of Failed Message Attachments – Salesforce_Satmetrix_Survey_I28
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We have an interface in CPI (Salesforce_Satmetrix_Survey_I28) where we are currently observing a high number of failures. The business has requested us to download all failed messages for further analysis.

At present, we are logging the source payload and HTTP response body from Satmetrix as attachments. The current requirement from the business is to download these attachments in bulk and share them for analysis.

Could you please assist with a way to extract and download these attachments in bulk at the earliest?

Please let us know if any additional details are required from our side.

Thanks & regards,
Eani sree.

entry.title: HTML5 (Custom Fiori) app fails with 500 (token exchange) in TST only – works in DEV & ACC landscape
entry.content:
We have a custom HTML5 app deployed to the HTML5 Application Repository and launched via managed Launchpad / Work Zone.

  •  DEV – works
  • ACC – works
  • TST – fails

Same MTA, same UI artifacts, same configuration pattern across all landscapes.

Problem (TST only)

  • /index.html
  • /manifest.json
  • /Component-preload.js

All return:
HTTP 500 – Internal Server Error Failed to exchange login token by an XSUAA token Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'url') identityzone undefined / clientid undefined

This happens for all users and occurs before any backend (CAP) call.

What is already verified

  1. HTML5 app is correctly uploaded and visible via cf html5-list
  2. Single app ID
  3. public visibility
  4. HTML5 repository destination exists and matches ACC:
  • sap.cloud.service
  • uri / content_endpoint
  • app_host_id
  • xsappname
  • OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • HTML5 repo host service key contains valid UAA data
  • Work Zone content provider updated
  • Same issue when launching via HTML5 Applications (not Work Zone specific)
  • Other HTML5 apps run fine in the same TST subaccount

Key observation
Since static files fail, this looks like a managed approuter / Launchpad runtime token‑exchange failure, not a CAP or UI build issue.

Has anyone seen a case where:

  • HTML5 repo content is present and public
  • Repository destination & service key are correct
  • DEV/ACC work
  • but one landscape fails with 500 during managed runtime token exchange?

Is this a known:

  • Launchpad runtime issue?
  • HTML5 repository runtime issue?
  • Internal managed destination / UAA resolution issue?
  • Any pointers, SAP Notes, or similar experience would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance 

Regards,
Raghav

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_schad 

since the Build Apps will soon no longer be part of SAP Build, I would ask you to remove the training content for SAP Build Apps from the official SAP learning hub. For instance, it is still part of the learning journey "Developing with SAP Build" with training course APP400:

Developing with SAP Build – From Apps to Automation

Does not make sense to keep it, also it may lead to unneccessary time investment by user who did not hear the news of SAP Build Apps depreciation and conduct the learning journey.

Thanks.

entry.title: Batch creation on material to material stock posting
entry.content:
Is it possible to create a new batch automatically on doing material to material movement. Currently 

Currently system is asking for receiving batch or copying the same batch

entry.title: Re: Cloud Transport Management for SAP BTP ABAP Environment – The Five-System Landscape
entry.content:
Hi Julie,

With Import Upto, does CTMS recognize the Git Branch and only imports the one and previous ones from the same Git Branch, or all regardless of Git Branch?

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Learning Hub Gets Smarter: AI-Assisted Learning Is Here
entry.content:
Building real SAP skills just got faster, more flexible, and more intelligent. SAP Learning Hub now brings AI-assisted learning directly into your experience, so you can learn, practice, and prepare for certification in a way that mirrors how SAP work actually gets done. 

At the heart of it is AI that meets you where you are. As a customer or partner using SAP Learning Hub, you can ask a question in natural language, in any of 80+ languages, and get an immediate, trusted answer drawn straight from SAP Learning Hub content. Prefer to learn through a mind map, podcast, or interactive Q&A? AI-supported formats adapt to how you learn best, making it easier to absorb and retain what matters.  Whatever your learning style, there is a format that works for you. 

The real impact shows up in practice. While working directly in SAP systems, you now have AI guidance on hand to help you explore, troubleshoot, and reinforce understanding in the flow of real work, not just in theory. It's the kind of support that builds genuine confidence, not just familiarity. 

Preparing for SAP Certification? Your preparation is now more closely aligned to performance-based exams, with AI-supported tools that reflect real project scenarios. AI-powered simulations and avatars give you a more realistic, engaging space to build confidence before stepping into live customer environments, so that when it matters, you’re ready.  

This is upskilling through SAP Learning Hub, designed for the way work is done today.  

Watch the feature overview video here and explore everything SAP Learning Hub has to offer: SAP Learning Hub | Your Online E-Learning Enablement Platform

entry.title: Re: SAP Composable Storefront – ASM - data isolation true - B2B site
entry.content:
Hi @balaji_k1,

we migrated, but the ASM still doesn't work.

The red banner disappeared, but when I try to search customers, this call: https://localhost:9002/assistedservicewebservices/customers/search?baseSite=... returns: 401 Unauthorized

If I watch in local.storage I do not see the access token.

Is  it correct?

This are the group of my agent.

Stefania

entry.title: Re: Button Feedback in SAP for Me doesn't work; how to al...
entry.content:
In fact, the below SAP note solves the issue although the symptom is not that I don't see the Feedback section, the symptom is that nothing happens when I click the Feedback button.

3402439 - Why do I not see the Feedback section in SAP for Me? - SAP for Me

In the left panel, click Legal > Cookie Preferences > Advanced Settings > Functional Cookies > "IN" for all > Set all to "OUT" except Qualtrics:

Now, it works:

entry.title: Re: Reconciliation acct (LFB1-AKONT) is a required entry...
entry.content:
Hello,

check if there is another company code with this data missing, indeed it is not your company code data in error but it is for another company code.

Romeo

entry.title: RBP Permissions have been affected with the new people profile.
entry.content:
The Role based permissions have been affected with the New People profile. It has made the information available to all the employees irrespective of the old permissions. Ideally, they should have taken care of the old permissions.

Also, when you wish to select the RBP in the Configure Latest People profile, it automatically selects that permission for all the roles, it doesn't even show which groups are getting affected.

It is really risky and there is no clarity on the configuration and the roles which are being impacted via the upgrade to Employee Profile.

As per the information on help.sap.com, RBPs were supposed to remain the same, but it seems like a lot has been impacted with the upgrade of people profile. Not at all happy with this.

entry.title: Limitation - Change in Availability of Dependents Infos for Onboarding Participants
entry.content:
Why was it decided or what is the reason for, that dependents can no longer be captured during the onboarding process? This represents a significant limitation for data collection by the onboardee in Switzerland. Providing information about children is mandatory during onboarding and cannot be postponed until the first working day, as subsequent processes depend on it. We would like to avoid having to rely on custom MDF objects instead of the standard object.

Change In Availability of Dependents Information | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud TRM-Special G/L indicator not defined or incorrect
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

I tried to reverse a loan in TRM, using the "Process Business Transactions" app, but I received the following error:

I've seen that in SAP On-Premise, the Special G/L flag can be deactivated using transaction /nob41 to revert the document, but in SAP Public Cloud, changes to the Posting Key cannot be made, as they are set by default.

Has anyone else encountered this problem?

I hope to receive some advice from you.

Thank you very much,

Dmytro.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code – Technical Series – Feature Flags Service
entry.content:
hi @natanielgubbins 

thanks for the feedback, ok good to know we can install the MTX to only works with feature toggles and not multi-tenancy

what is still not clear (with CAP and this NPM package), they don't talk about binding a service instance of Feature-Flags in your MTA, so how can you administer your feature flags from the application subscription dashboard? I am not interested about managing feature flags from package.json, we are interested about the dashboard experience.

FYI: the links now works because yesterday I opened an incident to SAP Support (trust me they were not working) 🙂

From SAP:  Thank you for reporting this issue! 
I've contacted our colleagues responsible for the documentation and the issue should be resolved now

I am interested on the binding approach, meaning having a CF reusable service instance of Feature Flags in my MTA (as a resources) and bound to my application service.

from there is not clear, in CAP, how to call the Feature Flags API, I guest reading the credentials from the VCAP application shared environment, not great documentation

thanks

entry.title: Re: How to Take SAP’s New Performance-Based Certification Exams
entry.content:
in the SuccessFactors's practical system-based exams, we are not able to find a scoring strategy or rubric to help us understand the results of our exams.  Previously, we received a breakdown of the percent questions correct by area.  Now, we only get the overall score.  We have opened a ticket with SAP and have been told that we will not receive a detailed breakdown of what was right/wrong.  I understand this logic - can anyone answer a basic question:  does a task have to be 100% correct or is there partial credit assigned?  In the test I took, there are tasks that have 1 main part, and there are those tasks that are multi-part.  Is the AI reviewing the coding of our templates or system to confirm final configuration for the whole task?  Is there partial credit?

It is difficult to study to retake an exam if you don't know at least what area was not 100%, but I understand the logic.  In a practical exam, like PM//GM/Dev/Calibration, perhaps at the very least, a specific module score breakdown would give us a learning path forward.

Thank you,
TKramer
